stories.title: Ravens cut Heisman Trophy runner-up Diego Pavia on eve of rookie camp
stories.summary: The Baltimore Ravens cut Heisman Trophy runner-up Diego Pavia on Thursday, just before rookie camp began. Pavia, who led Vanderbilt to its first 10-win season, had signed as an undrafted free agent in April. Veteran Skylar Thompson and rookie Joe Fagnano will now compete for the third quarterback spot.

stories.title: 'The Simpsons' returning to Fortnite next week. What we know
stories.summary: The Simpsons will return to Fortnite next week as part of a new "Simpsons Reload" game mode, which is a variation of the traditional Battle Royale game with smaller lobbies and maps. The mode is set to launch on Thursday, July 30, 2026, alongside the Fortnite v41.30 update, and will feature the Springfield map from Chapter 6: Mini Season 2. Players can expect to access the map after server maintenance downtime ends, likely in the morning of July 30.

stories.title: Men Flock to Microdramas, Romance Stories Rule and Sunday Nights are Prime Time for Viewing
stories.summary: Viewership of microdramas has surged 28-fold over the past two years, with more than two-thirds of users, both male and female, watching romance-based series. Sunday nights between 8-10 p.m. remain prime time for microdrama viewing, with heavy users consuming over three hours of content per week, including AI-generated material. This trend suggests a younger, more male audience is embracing the format, defying cultural concerns about AI-generated content.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US has imposed tariffs ranging from 10% to 12.5% on around 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, EU, Canada, Japan and India, over claims they failed to stop forced labour imports. The new duties, which come into effect on Friday, target key economic partners accounting for the vast majority of US imports. This move is the latest escalation in the global trade war, following the US Supreme Court's ruling that many previous tariffs were illegally enacted.

stories.title: Bertha weakens to tropical depression, but its moisture lives on, fueling a flood threat in the East
stories.summary: Bertha has weakened to a tropical depression, 10 miles north-northeast of Houston, but its moisture continues to fuel a flood threat in the East, with a plume of tropical moisture linked to the system bringing soaking rain to the Southeast. Heavy rain has prompted flash flood warnings in several states, including North Carolina, Virginia, and Tennessee, with some areas receiving a month's worth of rain in just a few days. The flooding threat is expected to continue through Friday, affecting areas from North and South Carolina to northern parts of Georgia and Alabama.

stories.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns defence boss
stories.summary: The UK's defence chief has warned that the country is complacent about the threat of foreign attack, with the level of threat being the highest he's seen in his lifetime. Defence spending must increase to meet the government's commitment to spend 3.5% of GDP on defence by 2035, with a focus on countering the use of autonomous lethal weapons by countries like Russia and Ukraine. This shift in modern warfare requires the UK to adapt its defence capabilities to prevent aggression and maintain human control over military operations.

stories.title: ‘Marvelous Mrs. Maisel’ Set Decorator Mary Kate Golding Fatally Stabbed in N.Y.
stories.summary: A 34-year-old set decorator for the TV show "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel" has been fatally stabbed in her Queens, New York apartment, with police responding to a 911 call at 12:24 am on July 21, 2026. The victim, Mary Kate Golding, was pronounced dead at the scene, and her husband, Joseph Azzaretto, was later found dead by hanging at Astoria Park, sparking a suspected murder-suicide investigation. The couple's baby son was found unharmed at their home.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: A record-breaking heatwave in England has left many residents, including stay-at-home mother Tavia Watts, feeling "incredibly helpless" with anxiety, with a 2022 study estimating that around 4.6% of people in the UK experience climate anxiety, a form of mental distress recognised by professional bodies and the NHS. Watts' emotional video on social media garnered over 18,000 likes and 1,700 reposts, highlighting the widespread concern. As temperatures continue to soar, experts warn that managing heatwave anxiety is crucial for those affected.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: A Singapore court has been working with Indonesian authorities to reunite 12 babies trafficked to the city-state with their biological families in Indonesia. The babies were part of a human trafficking ring that was recently convicted of selling at least 34 children for adoption. The reunification process is expected to be completed in the coming weeks, bringing closure to the adoptive parents in Singapore who have been waiting anxiously for a decision.

stories.title: Scientists find building blocks of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
stories.summary: Scientists have discovered the fundamental building blocks of birdsong, identifying eight key types of sound, known as "motifs", which are combined in various ways to create the incredible diversity of birdsong around the world. These motifs include trills, whistles, chaotic notes, and harmonies, and are found in over 116,000 songs from more than 3,000 bird species. The findings provide new insights into the complex communication systems of birds and could have significant implications for the study of animal behavior.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China's Moonshot AI has been accused of stealing capabilities from top US artificial intelligence models, including Anthropic's Fable AI, to develop its K3 model, according to US President Donald Trump's Science and Technology adviser Michael Kratsios. The alleged theft involved distillation, where a weaker AI model extracts answers from a stronger one, and access to restricted Nvidia servers for training. The US government claims to have information on Moonshot's activities, which could have significant implications for the global AI landscape.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals, the first major action under the EU's Digital Markets Act. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage, with a €460 million penalty for one breach and an additional €430 million for a second. Google will now have to strip away features and dismantle safety protections to comply with the EU's requirements.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A US health panel has narrowly recommended easing restrictions on several peptides, including BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals have not been proven safe or effective. The panel's 8-6 vote, with one abstention, is not binding but may influence the health secretary's decision to overturn FDA restrictions. If the FDA follows the advice, telehealth companies are expected to increase marketing of the peptides for wellness and cosmetic uses.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR inflicted more derby misery on Hull FC with a 28-20 win, ending their losing run and moving into fifth place in the Super League play-off positions. The away side came from behind to overcome a 12-0 lead by Hull FC, who have now lost six straight games and won just one of their past 11 matches. This victory extends Hull KR's winning run against their city rivals to nine in a row under coach Willie Peters.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended his 26-day hunger strike after the government assured no legal action would be taken against protesters at Janta Mantar site in India. This decision comes after state and health ministers visited Wangchuk in hospital, and mass protests continue in the Indian capital despite a brutal police crackdown on Monday's march to parliament. The youth movement, known as the Cockroach Jantar Mantar protest, demands the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: U.S. Withdraws Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times called an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Microsoft responds to LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows
stories.summary: Microsoft has responded to concerns over LG monitors installing McAfee ads on Windows without user permission, with some users finding the LG Monitor App Installer on their systems after purchasing the monitors. The app has been collecting user data, including geolocation and online activity, and has been installed via Windows Update. Microsoft has yet to comment on the specific case, but the incident raises questions about what Windows permits from peripherals' companion apps.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.
